pale-lamp.title: The Pale Lamp of the Leaning Tower
pale-lamp.theme: The long shadow of the Sundering on a frontier: wild magic creeping south into Halvedd's fields, and a tower that leans like the mind of the man who would not let his work die. Tragedy more than villainy -- the horror of a 'cure' that has quietly become the disease.
pale-lamp.boss: Maerith the Unfinished -- a magus of the fallen Aurelian Imperium who, on the night of the Sundering, lit the Pale Lamp in his tower's vault to ward Halvedd against the spreading catastrophe. He paid for the working with his life and would not accept it; he persists as a revenant bound to the Lamp -- lucid, courteous, and utterly certain he is still holding back a second Sundering. He is not evil. He is two centuries too late to stop, and his 'ward' is now what poisons the march.
pale-lamp.public: Known in-world: a grey blight is creeping into northern Halvedd from the Ashmarch -- crops greying, wells turning bitter, livestock born wrong, and the Whispering Stones louder than anyone living remembers. At night a cold blue light shows in the upper windows of the long-abandoned Leaning Tower on the Scar's edge. Boneland scavengers who went to loot it have not returned. Reeve Maddox of Wend and Brother Ossian of the Hermit's Hollow both want it looked into; Baron Thorn cannot afford to. Sages recall the tower was an imperial mage's, lost in the Sundering.
pale-lamp.secret: SEALED -- reveal only through play. (1) TRUE NATURE: the cold light is the Pale Lamp, a pre-Sundering wild-magic 'ward' Maerith lit to shield Halvedd. It worked, once. But the working was never finished -- Maerith lost his Focus (the Seventh Sigil, his staff-crystal) in the chaos before he could complete the binding rite, so the Lamp has burned unbalanced for two hundred years. (2) THE TWIST: the Lamp no longer wards anything. The catastrophe it was meant to hold back is long gone; the Lamp itself is now the source -- an open wild-magic wound that siphons the land to sustain Maerith's persistence and spills the grey blight south. He cannot perceive this; in his madness every blighted acre 'proves' the danger he must contain. (3) THE TRAP: smashing the Lamp by force triggers a wild-magic backlash (a real, dangerous burst -- roll it honestly) and only scatters the wound, so the blight worsens for a season before slowly settling. Brute force is a partial, costly 'win,' never a clean one. (4) WEAKNESS / THE KEY: Maerith is bound to the Lamp and to his lost Focus. Recover the Seventh Sigil (sealed in the tower's Focus Vault) and the unfinished rite can finally be completed AT the Lamp -- quenching it safely and laying Maerith to rest. Returning the Focus to him, or speaking the last line of his rite (reconstructed from his scattered journal), can also end him in peace -- and a party that grasps his tragedy may resolve the whole arc without a fight. (5) CLUE TRAIL: the lintel-glyph at the entrance (a ward, NOT a curse); Maerith's journal fragments in the Gallery, Glyph Hall, and Reliquary that name the Focus and the rite; Brother Ossian or a Sahli/League sage can identify the Sigil as an imperial focus-crystal; the Whispering Stones on the Ashmarch repeat the rite's opening words.
pale-lamp.win_condition: End the blight by quenching the Pale Lamp. CLEAN WIN: recover the Seventh Sigil from the Focus Vault, bring it to the Lamp Chamber, and either complete Maerith's rite or return his Focus to him -- laying him to rest and closing the wound; the grey creep recedes within weeks. COSTLY WIN: destroy the Lamp by force -- it works, but triggers a wild-magic backlash and the blight spikes before it fades. TALK-DOWN: a party that understands his tragedy can convince Maerith to release the rite himself. Readiness: a starting party, levels 1-3. This is the opening delve.
pale-lamp.clock: The grey creep spreads roughly one hex of Halvedd's farmland deeper each season the Lamp burns. Unchecked: by harvest, Wend's fields fail; within a year, refugees flow south and Valmoria offers Baron Thorn 'protection' he cannot refuse -- pulling the march toward Valmorian control and lighting one of the continent's cold tensions. As the clock advances the Lamp burns brighter, Maerith grows more active, and the dungeon grows more dangerous.
pale-lamp.notes: OPENING ARC. Set in the home region (Halvedd) and resolved in the Leaning Tower dungeon (engine/data/dungeon_leaning_tower.py; schematic at maps/leaning_tower.svg). Built for levels 1-3. Tone: tragic, not monstrous -- Maerith is courteous and reasonable, which should make laying him to rest land harder, not easier. Hooks out: success endears the party to Wend and Brother Ossian and forestalls the Valmoria lever; ignoring it advances the clock and the cold tension. Never reveal the twist except through the clue trail. Honest dice on the backlash.
